sanskrit:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा।
दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्॥
transliteration: na tu māṃ śakyase draṣṭum anenaiva sva-cakṣuṣā / divyaṃ dadāmi te cakṣuḥ paśya me yogam aiśvaram
literal_meaning: Not / indeed / Me / you are able / to see / with this / very / your own eye / divine / I give / to you / eye / see / My / mystic / majesty
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that his ordinary perception is insufficient to behold the full magnitude of reality; a transformative 'divine vision' must be granted. Psychologically, this mirrors the moment when a therapist or guide recognizes the client’s current cognitive-emotional frame cannot assimilate a traumatic or transcendent truth, so a new mode of seeing (reframing, symbolic language, or altered state) is temporarily provided.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative readiness—Arjuna is willing but lacks the internal structure to process overwhelming input; ego boundaries are still rigid.
trigger_scenario: A client about to enter trauma-focused EMDR or psychedelic-assisted therapy: intellectually consents, yet needs the therapist to ‘hold’ the frame so the mind can safely witness material it normally represses.
anchor_text: Open to a new way of seeing when your present eyes are not enough.
